description:
The syslog-ng service (in replacement of rsyslog) provides syslog-style logging by default on Debian. The syslog-ng service can be enabled with the following command: 
$ sudo systemctl enable syslog-ng.service
rationale:
The <tt>syslog-ng</tt> service must be running in order to provide
logging services, which are essential to system administration.
ocil_clause: the "syslog-ng" service is disabled, masked, or not started.
ocil:
Run the following command to determine the current status of the syslog-ng service: 
$ sudo systemctl is-active syslog-ng
If the service is running, it should return the following: 
active
fixtext:
To enable the syslog-ng service run the following command:
$ sudo systemctl enable --now syslog-ng
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 service syslog-ng must be enabled.
title: Enable syslog-ng Service
